title: FabricationCadConversionInterfaces
description: Shared cross-runtime JSON payloads for fabrication CAD, mesh, slicer, CAM setup, and neutral-export conversion workers. NATS subject names live in @dd/nats-subject-defs; these types describe the request and result bodies published on those subjects.

FabricationDesignInputRef.title: FabricationDesignInputRef
FabricationDesignInputRef.description: One reviewed design input handed to an external conversion worker. URIs should already be sanitized for logs and artifacts; proprietary source files remain operator-controlled references.

inputId.description: Stable designInputs id from the fabrication planning request, or a generated id when the original input was anonymous.

fileName.description: Original or normalized source filename, e.g. housing.sldprt, bracket.step, sculpt.blend, or setup.json.

sourceUri.description: Sanitized source location. Userinfo, query strings, and fragments must be removed before publishing this payload.

format.description: Declared or inferred source format token such as SLDPRT, SLDASM, PRT, ASM, STEP, STL, 3MF, DXF, FCStd, SCAD, BLEND, ZTL, or CAM_SETUP_JSON.

sourceSystem.description: Declared or inferred authoring system, e.g. SOLIDWORKS, PTC Creo, Siemens NX, CATIA, Fusion, Onshape, FreeCAD, OpenSCAD, Blender, ZBrush, PrusaSlicer, OrcaSlicer, Cura, or Bambu Studio.

role.description: Planning role for this input, for example editable native source CAD, verified neutral export candidate, slicer project evidence, or CAM setup evidence.

notes.description: Supplemental operator or planner notes retained as non-secret evidence.

sourceSha256.description: Optional SHA-256 of the referenced source object when the producer has already resolved the content.

FabricationDesignConversionTarget.title: FabricationDesignConversionTarget
FabricationDesignConversionTarget.description: One neutral-export or review artifact requested from conversion workers.

targetId.description: Stable target id used to correlate worker outputs with requested artifacts.

sourceInputId.description: Design input id this target should be generated from, when the target is input-specific.

format.description: Requested output or evidence format such as STEP, STL, 3MF, DXF, CAM_SETUP_JSON, SHEET_NESTING_JSON, SLICER_PROJECT, MESH_REPAIR_REPORT, or ASSEMBLY_GRAPH_JSON.

purpose.description: Why this target is needed, e.g. additive slicing, subtractive CAM, sheet nesting, assembly planning, simulation, or learning replay.

units.description: Requested unit system when known, e.g. mm or inch.

toleranceMm.description: Requested export tolerance in millimeters when the planner needs bounded geometry.

machineClass.description: Optional target machine class such as additive-fdm, additive-resin, vertical-mill, horizontal-mill, lathe, router, laser, waterjet, or plasma.

evidenceRequired.description: Evidence labels that must be returned before the planner can consider this artifact releasable.

FabricationDesignConversionBlocker.title: FabricationDesignConversionBlocker
FabricationDesignConversionBlocker.description: A release or conversion blocker reported by a planner or worker.

code.description: Stable blocker code, e.g. supported-native-cad-translator-required, ambiguous-native-extension, missing-units, non-manifold-mesh, or exporter-license-unavailable.

message.description: Human-readable blocker summary safe for operator logs.

inputId.description: Design input id associated with this blocker when applicable.

targetId.description: Requested target id associated with this blocker when applicable.

machineReadyImpact.description: Effect on release posture, e.g. blocks-machine-ready, needs-operator-review, or warning-only.

FabricationNeutralExportArtifact.title: FabricationNeutralExportArtifact
FabricationNeutralExportArtifact.description: One generated neutral export, slicer project, CAM setup, sheet nesting file, mesh report, or review artifact returned by a conversion worker.

artifactId.description: Stable generated artifact id retained by the planner.

targetId.description: Requested target id this artifact satisfies when known.

sourceInputId.description: Design input id used to create this artifact when known.

format.description: Generated format token such as STEP, STL, 3MF, DXF, CAM_SETUP_JSON, SHEET_NESTING_JSON, SLICER_PROJECT, MESH_REPAIR_REPORT, or ASSEMBLY_GRAPH_JSON.

uri.description: Sanitized artifact URI or object key. Do not include credentials, query strings, or fragments.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the generated artifact when available.

mediaType.description: MIME type or controlled media label for the artifact.

units.description: Artifact units when the format does not prove them unambiguously.

toleranceMm.description: Measured or exporter-declared tolerance in millimeters.

evidence.description: Evidence labels attached to this artifact, such as units-verified, mesh-manifold, watertight, profile-closed, slicer-profile-verified, or cam-setup-reviewed.

FabricationDesignConversionRequest.title: FabricationDesignConversionRequest
FabricationDesignConversionRequest.description: Body published on dd.remote.fabrication.design.conversion.requests for external CAD, mesh, slicer, CAM setup, and neutral-export workers.

schema.description: Envelope schema id. Producers should emit dd.fabrication.design-conversion.request.v1.

requestId.description: Unique id for this conversion job, suitable for dedupe and result correlation.

planRequestId.description: Fabrication planning request id that spawned this conversion job.

jobId.description: Optional fabrication job id when the planner has already persisted one.

emittedAtMs.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the planner published this request.

producer.description: Producer service instance or deployment name.

resultSubject.description: Subject workers should publish the result to; defaults to dd.remote.fabrication.design.conversion.results.

designInputs.description: Reviewed source inputs that need conversion, export, repair, or review evidence.

targets.description: Requested STEP/STL/3MF/DXF/CAM setup/sheet nesting targets, slicer projects, mesh reports, and assembly graphs requested from workers.

blockers.description: Planner-known blockers that workers may clear by returning verified artifacts or evidence.

priority.description: Optional scheduling hint. Higher values mean more urgent conversion work.

notes.description: Non-secret planning notes for the worker.

FabricationDesignConversionResult.title: FabricationDesignConversionResult
FabricationDesignConversionResult.description: Body published on dd.remote.fabrication.design.conversion.results with generated artifacts, translator evidence, blockers, and machine-ready posture.

schema.description: Envelope schema id. Workers should emit dd.fabrication.design-conversion.result.v1.

requestId.description: Conversion request id this result answers.

planRequestId.description: Fabrication planning request id from the original conversion job.

jobId.description: Optional persisted fabrication job id from the original request.

emittedAtMs.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the worker published this result.

workerId.description: Worker id, pod name, or adapter instance that produced this result.

translator.description: Translator, exporter, slicer, mesh repair, or CAM adapter used.

translatorVersion.description: Translator or adapter version used for repeatability and learning attribution.

success.description: True when the worker completed its requested conversion or review work.

machineReady.description: True only when the returned artifacts and evidence are sufficient for the planner to consider the conversion lane releasable.

artifacts.description: Generated artifacts and review evidence returned by the worker.

blockers.description: Remaining blockers or new worker-discovered blockers.

warnings.description: Non-blocking warnings that should be retained with the design evidence.

reviewMetadata.description: Free-form non-secret metadata such as source configuration, Onshape document/version/element ids, exporter settings, repair statistics, or CAM/slicer profile names.
